description: You are a traffic data analyst at TPER (Trasporto Passeggeri Emilia-Romagna), Bologna's transit and traffic authority. The Acosta corridor's traffic demand model needs recalibration against real detector data. Using the Bologna Acosta scenario at /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_acosta/, generate baseline vehicle counts from the simulation and save them to /home/ga/SUMO_Output/baseline_detector_counts.csv with columns: detector_id, interval_begin, interval_end, vehicle_count. Create a synthetic 'observed' reference dataset by applying a systematic scaling factor to the baseline counts and save it to /home/ga/SUMO_Output/observed_detector_counts.csv using the same format. Calibrate the traffic demand by modifying the route file to match these observed counts, saving the new route file to /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_acosta/acosta_calibrated.rou.xml and the simulation configuration to /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_acosta/run_calibrated.sumocfg. Extract the calibrated simulation counts to /home/ga/SUMO_Output/calibrated_detector_counts.csv. Produce a calibration assessment report at /home/ga/SUMO_Output/calibration_report.csv with columns: detector_id, observed_count, baseline_count, calibrated_count, baseline_geh, calibrated_geh. Document the calibration methodology and GEH statistics in a summary at /home/ga/SUMO_Output/calibration_summary.txt.
